title: Selfish
wikitext:
[]: Selfish? *MASSIVE SPOILERS*             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon Dec 28, 2009 4:27 pm When Shinji was about to tear Rei out of Zeruel's body, someone mentions this is a selfish gesture on the scale of EoE. I think that's a great, steaming pile of horseshit. In EoE, Shinji is basically a shattered, broken boy in no condition to fight. He's a wreck that can't even bring himself to suicide. Shinji even says outright that "Nobody loves me, so they can all just DIE." In Rebuild, however, Shinji attacks Zeruel with nearly suicidal courage. He willingly puts himself in harms way not for his own benefit, but to save Rei. He say what Zeruel did to everything else that was in its way, and went out to fight of his own volition. That screams selflessness to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Dec 28, 2009 4:35 pm It isn't selfish at all. If Shinji was being selfish in the finale he would just act like a chicken shit and let Rei be absorbed, have doubts as he did it, question if he should, etc. take your pic. He risked his own life and didn't care about what happened to him. As long as Rei was alright.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Dec 28, 2009 4:39 pm ^ and screaming at the same time "screw teh world" not as selfish as when in his runtness in EoE but isn't exactly the paragon of virtue either

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon Dec 28, 2009 4:39 pm "I don't care what happens to the world," doesn't suggest the purest of motives. I think there's underlying similarities with Gendo in the final scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Dec 28, 2009 4:44 pm Forgot about that...

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon Dec 28, 2009 4:51 pm Lucretius wrote: "I don't care what happens to the world," doesn't suggest the purest of motives. I think there's underlying similarities with Gendo in the final scene.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Dec 28, 2009 7:18 pm Being willing to destroy the whole world to save someone you care about is still selfish. Or do I need to bring up Gendo and Yui?

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Mon Dec 28, 2009 10:04 pm Xard wrote: ^ and screaming at the same time "screw teh world"

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Dec 28, 2009 10:08 pm It doesn't really matter: the intent and will was there. If Shinji was presented with options A) screw the world for lulz B) save Rei he'd save Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Dec 28, 2009 10:48 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Being willing to destroy the whole world to save someone you care about is still selfish. Or do I need to bring up Gendo and Yui?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Dec 29, 2009 12:43 am You say that as if Shinji had any idea he was about to cause third impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Tue Dec 29, 2009 2:47 am It gets down to, does he want to save Ayanami for Ayanami's sake, or does he want to save her for his own sake? The more likely answer is indeterminable parts of both, making at least somewhat an inherently selfish gesture.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Tue Dec 29, 2009 3:12 am Teague wrote: It gets down to, does he want to save Ayanami for Ayanami's sake, or does he want to save her for his own sake? The more likely answer is indeterminable parts of both, making at least somewhat an inherently selfish gesture.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Dec 29, 2009 6:58 am Even if he purely did it for Rei and had no self-directed intent whatsoever, he's still willing to destroy and forsake the world for her regardless of her own feelings. Selfish.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Dec 29, 2009 10:48 am lol glad you understood what I ment even though I totally f*cked up that post...

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Tue Dec 29, 2009 9:42 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Being willing to destroy the whole world to save someone you care about is still selfish. Or do I need to bring up Gendo and Yui?

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Thu Dec 31, 2009 12:16 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Which is exactly why I brought that up in a recent thread in Rebuild. Besides, I'm not the only one to make the Shinji=selfish observation, and while it's not on the same scale as Shinji's actions in EoE with having an actual death-wish for everybody (making humanity lose their AT Fields), it's still pretty bad Shinji wanted to screw the world for Rei's sake anyway. Xard: Waha, I was gonna cross-post with you. @_@ But yeah, exactly.
